message:
fix(net): use wall_now() time base in Happy Eyeballs timeout, fix tls import order

The RaceConnections timeout sleep used a separate WallClock epoch that
drifted from the wall_now() time base Sleep::poll() falls back to when
no Cx is available. This caused the timeout to fire immediately in test
suites where START_TIME was initialized much earlier than WallClock,
making race_connections_all_fail return TimedOut instead of the expected
ConnectionRefused.

Fix: use crate::time::wall_now() as the fallback in timeout_now() so
deadline computation and sleep polling share the same monotonic epoch.

Also fix rustfmt import ordering in tls/types.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
